doc-block.comment: A `DOC ... END DOC` documentation block. The keyword line may carry only whitespace-separated attribute words (e.g. `DOC INTERNAL`); the body is captured verbatim by the lexer and is not tokenized as code.
doc-block.name: comment.block.documentation.mfbasic

patterns.comment: An EXAMPLE region inside a DOC block holds real MFBASIC source; re-inject the grammar so it highlights.
REM comment — only at the start of a statement (line start or after a `:` separator). Case-insensitive. Group 1 covers REM through end of line so the whole remark is colored.

strings.comment: Double-quoted string. Not multiline — ends at the closing quote or at end of line (recovery for an unterminated literal).
strings.name: string.quoted.double.mfbasic

patterns.comment: Recognized escapes: \" \\ \n \t. Any other backslash escape simply drops the backslash in the lexer.
patterns.name: constant.character.escape.mfbasic

numbers.comment: Integer or decimal literal. No hex/binary/exponent/underscore/suffix forms exist in the lexer. A leading minus is the unary operator, not part of the literal.
numbers.name: constant.numeric.mfbasic

end-block.comment: `END <modifier>` closes a block. Color the `END` with the SAME scope as its companion keyword so the pair renders as one unit. Declaration terminators (END FUNC/SUB/TYPE/ENUM/UNION) take keyword.declaration to match FUNC/TYPE/...; control terminators (END IF/MATCH/TRAP/WITH) take keyword.control to match IF/.... A bare `END`, or `END` with any other word (e.g. END LINK), falls through to the generic control-keyword rule.

patterns.comment: Declarations and clause keywords. Their own keyword.declaration scope (NOT keyword.control) so a theme can tint them differently from control flow. They no longer carry storage.type, which themes paint with the TYPE color.
patterns.name: keyword.declaration.mfbasic

builtin-types.comment: Primitive, special, and template type names.
builtin-types.name: support.type.builtin.mfbasic

qualified-call.comment: A `package::name` reference. The package prefix is a namespace; `::` is a scope operator; a trailing name followed by `(` is a call.

function-call.comment: An identifier immediately followed by `(` is a call.

user-types.comment: Convention: types are CapitalCamelCase (a capital followed by at least one lowercase letter). Keywords are matched earlier, so this only reaches user/record type names.
user-types.name: entity.name.type.mfbasic

line-continuation.comment: A trailing `_` (preceded by whitespace, followed only by whitespace to end of line) continues the statement on the next line.
line-continuation.name: keyword.operator.continuation.mfbasic
